items.claim: Max Holloway's speed, footwork, and punch volume are his primary advantages as a fighter.
items.quote: He's an accumulative puncher. He throws punches and bunches. He throws combinations. He flows. He moves. He uses his footwork. He's quick. He's rhythmic.

items.claim: Conor McGregor possesses significantly more power than Max Holloway.
items.quote: He doesn't possess the same power as Connor. We saw that in their first fight. He just doesn't.

items.claim: Moving the fight to 170 lbs negatively impacts Max Holloway by neutralizing his speed and movement advantages.
items.quote: When you make this fight at 170, you take away Max's speed. You take away all his best advantages.

items.claim: Max Holloway and Conor McGregor are both naturally 145-pound fighters whose best performances occur at that weight class.
items.quote: These guys are true, in my opinion, true 145 pound fighters that they've aged in the game and they've moved up a weight, but they're best versions of themsel at competition level as 145 lbs.

items.claim: Increasing body weight generally improves a fighter's durability and power while decreasing their speed, movement, and punch volume.
items.quote: When I get heavier, I'm more durable. I'm more strong. I'm more powerful. But I throw maybe little less punches. I think I move a little bit less.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has used performance-enhancing drugs during his time away from the sport.
items.quote: it doesn't take away from the fact that he's admitted that that has been used on the outside to get to where he's at now.
